skill.name: dotnet-backend
skill.description: Build ASP.NET Core 8+ backend services with EF Core, auth, background jobs, and production API patterns.
skill.summary: Enterprise-grade ASP.NET Core API development with Entity Framework, JWT authentication, and background services.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static analyzer findings were false positives. The backtick patterns detected were markdown code fences around C# examples, not shell execution. The cryptographic and reconnaissance flags matched unrelated documentation text. The skill provides legitimate ASP.NET Core backend development guidance with no security concerns.

content.user_title: Build production-ready ASP.NET Core APIs with expert guidance
content.value_statement: Struggling with .NET backend complexity? This skill delivers enterprise-grade API patterns, Entity Framework optimization, and secure authentication implementations out of the box.
actual_capabilities: Build RESTful APIs using Minimal APIs or controller-based patterns
Implement JWT authentication and policy-based authorization
Configure Entity Framework Core with optimized query patterns
Create background services using IHostedService and BackgroundService
Apply production patterns including exception handling, validation, and logging
limitations: Focused on ASP.NET Core 8+; .NET Framework projects require different approaches
Backend-only scope; frontend implementation not included
Cloud deployment configuration requires explicit request

use_cases.title: API Development
use_cases.description: Build new REST APIs from scratch with proper architecture, authentication, and data access patterns

use_cases.title: Legacy Modernization
use_cases.description: Refactor existing .NET code to modern ASP.NET Core patterns with improved performance

use_cases.title: Authentication Implementation
use_cases.description: Add secure JWT-based auth, role management, and policy-based authorization
use_cases.target_user: Developers implementing secure API access control

prompt_templates.title: Create a new API endpoint
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Minimal API endpoint for managing products with GET all, GET by id, POST, PUT, and DELETE operations. Use EF Core with a Product entity and include proper validation.

prompt_templates.title: Add JWT authentication
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up JWT authentication in my ASP.NET Core app. Include token generation service, validation configuration, and protect specific endpoints with the Authorize attribute.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize database queries
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my EF Core queries for N+1 problems and suggest optimizations using Include, ThenInclude, AsNoTracking, and projection patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Build a background job processor
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a BackgroundService that processes queued email notifications every 5 minutes. Use scoped services, proper error handling, and graceful shutdown with CancellationToken.

output_examples.input: Create a user registration endpoint with email validation and password hashing
output_examples.output: Provides a complete Minimal API endpoint with FluentValidation for email format, BCrypt password hashing, EF Core user creation, and proper HTTP status codes (201 Created, 400 Bad Request)

output_examples.input: How do I implement role-based access control?
output_examples.output: Delivers a policy-based authorization setup with custom requirements, handlers, role configuration in appsettings, and [Authorize(Policy = 'AdminOnly')] attribute usage examples

best_practices: Always use async/await for I/O operations to maximize scalability
Store secrets in User Secrets during development and Key Vault in production
Implement global exception handling middleware for consistent error responses
anti_patterns: Blocking on async code with .Result or .Wait() causes deadlocks
Returning entities directly instead of DTOs exposes internal structure
Hardcoding connection strings instead of using configuration

faq.question: What .NET version does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on ASP.NET Core 8+ and the latest .NET runtime. Older .NET Framework patterns may require adjustments.

faq.question: Can this skill help with database migrations?
faq.answer: Yes, it covers EF Core Code-First migrations including Add-Migration, Update-Database commands, and migration troubleshooting.

faq.question: Does this skill support both Minimal APIs and Controllers?
faq.answer: Yes, it provides guidance for both Minimal API patterns (new in .NET 6+) and traditional controller-based architectures.

faq.question: What authentication methods are covered?
faq.answer: JWT bearer tokens, ASP.NET Core Identity, OAuth 2.0 flows, and integration with Azure AD are all supported.

faq.question: Can this skill help with API performance optimization?
faq.answer: Yes, it covers response caching, output caching, connection pooling, query optimization, and async patterns for better throughput.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for microservices development?
faq.answer: Yes, it includes patterns for building independent services with proper boundaries, health checks, and inter-service communication via gRPC or REST.
